title: Gochujang Tuna & Sweetcorn Air Fryer Jacket Potato Recipe
summary: Elevate your air fryer jacket potato with a bold and flavourful twist by adding a Gochujang Tuna & Sweetcorn filling. This recipe combines the ease of air frying with the spicy-sweet depth of Gochujang to create a satisfying meal that’s both quick and delicious. The crispy skin of the baked potato, achieved through the air fryer, contrasts beautifully with the creamy, spicy tuna mixture, making it a unique take on the classic jacket potato. Perfect for a hearty lunch or dinner, this dish is as versatile as it is tasty. Whether you're using a white potato or sweet potatoes, the air fryer ensures they come out perfectly every time. Garnish with spring onions or a sprinkle of cabbage for added crunch and freshness. This air fryer jacket potato recipe is sure to impress with its combination of textures and flavours, turning a simple potato into a gourmet experience.

method.description: Prick the potatoes all over with a fork, then drizzle with 2 tsp oil and season with salt and pepper. Transfer to the basket of the air fryer and set the temperature to 200°C. Set the timer for 45 mins and cook until the skin is crisp, but a sharp knife slides into the middle easily.
Meanwhile, very thinly slice the spring onions and scrape three-quarters into a bowl. Roughly chop three-quarters of the coriander and scrape that into the bowl, too.

Drain the cans of tuna and sweetcorn and add to the bowl with the spring onions and coriander, then spoon in the mayo, gochujang and rice vinegar. Season with a little salt and pepper and gently fold together. Taste and adjust the seasoning, adding more gochujang if you want it spicier.

Put the jacket potatoes onto a plate and cut down the middle, gently pressing the sides to open it up. Season the inside with salt and pepper, then divide the tuna and sweetcorn mix over the potatoes.
Sprinkle over the reserved spring onions and coriander leaves and the sesame seeds. Serve with kimchi on the side.

chefs.name: Adam Bush
chefs.summary: Adam is a chef, baker and food writer with a love of big, bold flavours. He trained in Michelin-starred kitchens, pubs and a bakery before turning his hand to recipe writing.
